Analysis of the specific price levels for ground invasion targets, as the current efficiency checks focused almost exclusively on strike/missile exchange markets (Iran vs Israel, UK/UAE vs Iran) rather than the requested ground troop escalation.
completenessCheck.suggestedFix: The initial search identified the correct markets (e.g., slugs 'us-forces-enter-iran-by' and 'will-the-us-invade-iran-in-2025'), but they were not passed to the data-fetching tools. Execute 'search_and_get_outcomes' or 'get_event_outcomes' for these specific slugs to retrieve the actual price levels and implied odds. Then, run 'check_market_efficiency' on those specific ground-invasion markets to fulfill the user's request for odds and price levels.

blockingDiagnostics.message: Detected excessive same-tool discovery fanout; use one broad discovery/batch call, build a shortlist, then run at most 1-2 sequential follow-ups.

responsePreview:
Based on recent Polymarket data, several markets track escalation risks involving Iran. While specific "boots on the ground" markets have been identified, the current data provides implied odds primarily for military strikes rather than ground invasions.

### **Implied Odds for Iran Military Action**
The following markets have been analyzed for efficiency and current pricing levels:

| Market Title | Implied Probability (YES) | Resolution Date | Status |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Will Iran strike Safaniya Field by April 30? | 20% | April 30, 2026 | Live |
| Will UK strike Iran by April 30? | 6.5% | April 30, 2026 | Live |
| Will UAE strike Iran by March 31? | 5.5% | March 31, 2026 | Live |
| Will Iran strike Israel by April 30, 2026? | 100% (High Vig)* | April 30, 2026 | Live |
| Will Iran strike Israel in March? | 100% (High Vig)* | March 31, 2026 | Live |

*\*Note: Markets with 100% implied probability and 50% "NO" prices indicate poor efficiency and high vig (50%); these may not accurately reflect real-world probability.*

### **Direct "Boots on the Ground" Markets Identified**
The following markets directly track ground escalation, though current price levels were not inc
